title: Cheap Houses for Rent in Hough, Cleveland
metaTitle: Cheap Houses for Rent in Hough, Cleveland OH
metaDescription: Cheap houses for rent in Hough, Cleveland (44103). Compare typical rent by bedroom count, income limits for vouchers, and book a free showing.
primaryKeyword: cheap houses for rent Hough cleveland
answerBox: Hough, on Cleveland's east side in ZIP 44103, offers some of the more affordable rental houses on the city's east side, with 2-bedroom homes generally running $750 to $1,050 a month. Rent Finder Cleveland works with voucher-friendly homes throughout the neighborhood, and every property accepts Section 8.

sections.h2: Finding a cheap house to rent in Hough
body: Hough sits in **ZIP code 44103** on Cleveland's east side, positioned between Downtown and University Circle with Euclid Avenue running along its northern edge. Much of the neighborhood's housing stock dates to the early 1900s — smaller wood-frame single-family homes and two-family duplexes on modest lots — which generally rent for less than Cleveland's newer, larger apartment developments.

Rent Finder Cleveland works with voucher-friendly rental homes in and around Hough. [Book a free showing](/p/book-showing) to check current availability, or tell our leasing team your budget and we'll match you with options as they open up.

sections.h2: What does a cheap rent look like in Hough right now?
body: Two-bedroom homes in Hough generally rent from the mid-$700s to about $1,050 a month, and three-bedroom homes run roughly $1,050 to $1,400 — both well below RentCafe's July 2, 2026 citywide apartment-building average of $1,564/month, since that figure is weighted toward newer, larger complexes rather than Hough's older housing stock.

rows: Most common find for budget-focused renters
Still below RentCafe's citywide apartment average

sections.h2: Keeping costs down: utilities and transit in Hough
body: Electric service in Hough comes from either Cleveland Public Power (CPP), the city-owned utility, or The Illuminating Company, depending on the exact address — worth checking before you sign, since rates differ. Natural gas is generally supplied by Enbridge Gas Ohio (formerly Dominion East Ohio), and Cleveland Water handles the water line. The Euclid Avenue HealthLine bus rapid transit route runs directly through the neighborhood, which can reduce or eliminate the need for a car and its associated costs for renters who work or study near downtown or University Circle.

items: **Electric:** Cleveland Public Power or The Illuminating Company, depending on address
**Gas:** Enbridge Gas Ohio (formerly Dominion East Ohio) in most of the city
**Transit:** Euclid Avenue HealthLine (BRT) reduces the need for a car

sections.h2: Section 8 and other low-cost paths to renting in Hough
body: Every home Rent Finder Cleveland works with in Hough accepts Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) and is HUD-inspection-ready. Vouchers are administered by the Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority (CMHA), which accepts preliminary applications year-round with no closing date; as of CMHA's income limits effective April 3, 2025, a single person must earn no more than $34,800 a year and a 4-person household no more than $49,700 to qualify. Selection runs through periodic lottery draws rather than strict order of application. See our full [Cleveland Section 8 guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) for the complete process.

sections.h2: Protecting your budget: what Ohio law says about deposits
body: Keeping a cheap rental cheap also means understanding your security deposit rights. Ohio law generally requires a landlord to return a tenant's deposit within 30 days after the lease ends and the tenant moves out and provides a forwarding address, along with an itemized list of any deductions, under Ohio Revised Code 5321.16. If the deposit exceeds $50 or one month's rent (whichever is greater) and the tenant stayed six months or more, the landlord generally owes 5% annual interest on the excess amount. A tenant who believes a deposit was wrongfully withheld may be able to recover double the amount plus attorney's fees under the same statute. This is general information, not legal advice — verify current details with the [Ohio Revised Code](https://codes.ohio.gov/ohio-revised-code/section-5321.16) or a local tenant-rights organization.

sections.h2: Comparing Hough to other affordable Cleveland neighborhoods
body: If a specific budget rental isn't open in Hough when you search, it's worth comparing similarly-priced options in [cheap houses in Slavic Village](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/cheap-houses-for-rent-slavic-village-cleveland/), another east-side neighborhood where Rent Finder Cleveland works with voucher-friendly homes. If a three-bedroom layout matters more than price alone, our [3-bedroom Hough guide](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/3-bedroom-houses-for-rent-hough-cleveland/) breaks down typical sizes and features in the same neighborhood, and you can always [apply online](/apply) once you find a home you like.

faq.q: Are there cheap houses for rent in Hough?
faq.a: Yes. Rent Finder Cleveland works with voucher-friendly rental homes throughout Hough, where 2-bedroom homes typically run $750 to $1,050 a month. Book a showing to see current availability.

faq.q: How much do 2-bedroom houses rent for in Hough?
faq.a: Two-bedroom homes in Hough generally rent from the mid-$700s to about $1,050 a month, well below Cleveland's average for newer professionally-managed apartment buildings.

faq.q: Does Hough accept Section 8 housing vouchers?
faq.a: Every home Rent Finder Cleveland works with in Hough accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready. Acceptance from other individual landlords varies, since Ohio has no statewide requirement and Cleveland has no source-of-income ordinance.

faq.q: What are the income limits for a CMHA housing voucher?
faq.a: As of CMHA's income limits effective April 3, 2025, a single person must earn no more than $34,800 a year and a 4-person household no more than $49,700 to qualify for a Housing Choice Voucher. Limits are updated annually by HUD.

faq.q: What other affordable neighborhoods does Rent Finder Cleveland serve?
faq.a: Our voucher-friendly homes are concentrated on Cleveland's east and southeast side, including Hough, Slavic Village, Collinwood, and Glenville, with a smaller presence on the west side. Reach out with your budget for current options across these areas.

faq.q: How long does a landlord have to return my deposit in Ohio?
faq.a: Ohio law generally requires landlords to return a tenant's security deposit within 30 days after lease end and move-out, along with an itemized statement of any deductions, under Ohio Revised Code 5321.16. This is general information, not legal advice — verify current details before signing or moving out.
